questionHtml:
Don took part in a project investigating wind speed, x km h-1, and the time, y minutes, to fully charge a solar powered robot.

The investigation was carried out six times. The results are recorded in the table.

M is the point with coordinates (x, y).

Don concluded from his investigation: “There is no causation between wind speed and the time to fully charge the robot”.

In the context of the question, briefly explain the meaning of “no causation”.
markschemeHtml:
wind speed does not cause a change in the time to charge (the robot)      (A1)


Note: Award (A1) for a statement that communicates the meaning of a non-causal relationship between the two variables.


[1 mark]

meta.title: Don concluded from his investigation: “There is no causation between wind speed and the time to fully charge the robot”. In the context of the question, briefly explain the meaning
breadcrumbLabels: SL 4.4—Pearsons, scatter diagrams, eqn of y on x
